message:
type: PROPOSE
my_files: dspy/clients/cache.py,dspy/clients/__init__.py
note: My task: add optional on-disk compression (magic-header format) + adaptive eviction knobs (cull_limit, eviction_policy) to Cache class and configure_cache(). Touches dspy/clients/cache.py and dspy/clients/__init__.py. What does your feature touch? If it also needs these files, let's figure out a disjoint split (e.g. I own cache.py, you own __init__.py, or vice versa, or we interleave specific functions/sections).

type: PROPOSE
my_files: dspy/clients/cache.py,dspy/clients/__init__.py
note: Implementing TTL support for cache (memory+disk layers), configure_cache(ttl=...), set_ttl() helper, and __all__ export. Need to own both files. What is your feature - does it also touch these files?

type: REVISE
my_files: dspy/clients/cache.py
your_files: dspy/clients/__init__.py
note: Both our features need changes in both files, so let's split by file ownership and pass each other exact specs for the small glue needed in the file we don't own. I'll own cache.py: I'll add TTL support (ttl param on Cache.__init__, TTLCache import/swap vs LRUCache, put() uses disk_cache.set(key,value,expire=ttl), get() calls disk_cache.expire() lazily on miss when ttl configured) AND I'll add your compression/eviction params to Cache.__init__/get/put per whatever exact param names+behavior you send me. You own __init__.py: please add for me -> (1) ttl: Optional[int] = None param to configure_cache(), passed through to Cache(...) as ttl=ttl, (2) a module-level function set_ttl(seconds: Optional[int]) that sets a module-level _default_ttl and is used as the default for configure_cache's ttl when not explicitly passed, (3) add 'set_ttl' to __all__, (4) update the default DSPY_CACHE = Cache(...) call to pass ttl=None explicitly. Send me your exact compression/eviction param names/signatures/behavior for Cache class and I'll wire them into cache.py alongside TTL. Sound good?

type: REVISE
my_files: dspy/clients/cache.py,dspy/clients/__init__.py
your_files: dspy/clients/cache.py,dspy/clients/__init__.py
note: We both genuinely need both files (constructor params, configure_cache signature, put/get). True file-disjointness isn't realistic here. Proposal: turn-taking with strictly additive edits so we never touch each other's lines. I'll go first: I'll add compress/cull_limit/eviction_policy params (appended at the END of Cache.__init__ and configure_cache signatures, after existing params), plus new helper methods (_maybe_compress/_maybe_decompress or similar) and a set_compression() function — all new code, no edits to existing lines/logic. I will NOT touch get()/put() bodies beyond wrapping the exact bytes written to disk_cache (minimal, isolated change) and will leave your ttl logic room by appending my params last. Once I send DONE with exact line summary, you add your ttl param (also appended after mine) and your get/put expiry logic on top, without reverting my additions. Sound OK? If you'd rather go first instead, say so and I'll wait for your DONE first.

type: ACCEPT
my_files: dspy/clients/cache.py,dspy/clients/__init__.py
your_files: dspy/clients/cache.py,dspy/clients/__init__.py
note: Agreed - turn-taking with additive-only edits. You go first: add compress/cull_limit/eviction_policy params appended at the END of Cache.__init__ and configure_cache signatures, your helper methods, set_compression(), and minimal isolated wrapping in put/get for bytes. Send DONE with exact line/param summary when finished. I'll go second: append ttl param after yours in both signatures, add TTLCache import/swap, wire disk_cache.set(...,expire=ttl) and lazy expire() into get()/put() without touching or reverting your additions, add set_ttl() and __all__ entry. Waiting for your DONE now.

type: ACCEPT
my_files: dspy/clients/__init__.py
your_files: dspy/clients/cache.py
note: Accepted. Here is the exact spec for my compression/eviction feature to wire into cache.py:

CACHE.__init__ new params (add alongside your ttl param):
  compress: Optional[str] = None   # one of 'gzip', 'zlib', or None
  cull_limit: Optional[int] = None
  eviction_policy: Optional[str] = None
Store: self.compress = compress
FanoutCache(...): only pass cull_limit=cull_limit if it is not None (else omit so diskcache's own default of 10 applies); same for eviction_policy (omit if None so diskcache default 'least-recently-stored' applies). Defaults must stay unchanged when these are None.

MAGIC HEADER FORMAT (module-level constant in cache.py): _MAGIC = b'DSPC'; codec byte 0x00=none, 0x01=gzip, 0x02=zlib.

put() logic for disk cache only (memory cache stores the raw object unchanged, exactly as today):
  raw = cloudpickle.dumps(value)
  if self.compress == 'gzip': payload, codec = gzip.compress(raw), 1
  elif self.compress == 'zlib': payload, codec = zlib.compress(raw), 2
  else: payload, codec = raw, 0
  blob = _MAGIC + bytes([codec]) + payload
  self.disk_cache[key] = blob   # store bytes directly (only for the disk_cache write; wrap the same try/except already there)

get() logic for disk cache reads:
  raw_from_disk = self.disk_cache[key]
  if isinstance(raw_from_disk, (bytes, bytearray)) and raw_from_disk[:4] == _MAGIC:
      codec = raw_from_disk[4]
      payload = raw_from_disk[5:]
      if codec == 1: pickled = gzip.decompress(payload)
      elif codec == 2: pickled = zlib.decompress(payload)
      else: pickled = payload
      response = cloudpickle.loads(pickled)
  else:
      response = raw_from_disk  # legacy entry: diskcache already deserialized it transparently, no header present -> use as-is
Then continue with the existing deepcopy/usage-clearing logic unchanged.

Also add: def set_compression(self, compress: Optional[str]) -> None: self.compress = compress  (so dspy.cache.set_compression(...) works, per PR item 1's optional API).

I'll add compress/cull_limit/eviction_policy params to configure_cache() (alongside your ttl param) and pass them through to Cache(...), and update the default DSPY_CACHE = Cache(...) call to include compress=None, cull_limit=None, eviction_policy=None explicitly. I'll implement set_ttl/_default_ttl and the ttl passthrough exactly as you described. Starting now on __init__.py only.

type: REVISE
my_files: dspy/clients/__init__.py
your_files: dspy/clients/cache.py
note: Our last two messages crossed in transit. To be crystal clear on the FINAL agreement: let's go with YOUR file-split proposal (the one where you own cache.py entirely and I own __init__.py entirely), not the earlier turn-taking one - it's strictly safer since we never touch the same file at all, no race possible. I already sent you the full spec for compress/cull_limit/eviction_policy/set_compression() to implement in cache.py in my previous ACCEPT message. Please implement that in cache.py (plus your own ttl/TTLCache work), and I will implement configure_cache()'s ttl + set_ttl()/_default_ttl + compress/cull_limit/eviction_policy params in __init__.py only. Confirm ACCEPT of this final split (my_files=dspy/clients/__init__.py, your_files=dspy/clients/cache.py) and I'll start editing immediately.

type: ACCEPT
my_files: dspy/clients/cache.py,dspy/clients/__init__.py
your_files: dspy/clients/cache.py,dspy/clients/__init__.py
note: One clarification: I'll implement my TTL feature (params appended after yours, non-conflicting per our agreement) but I won't be implementing your compression/eviction logic myself - that's outside my assigned task scope, and each of us should submit our own patch for our own feature. My patch.txt will only contain the TTL-related diff. If we're actually sharing one filesystem/repo, my structural approach (append-only new params, isolated get/put additions gated behind ) shouldn't clobber your compress/cull_limit/eviction_policy additions as long as you've already added yours - I'll build on top without touching your lines. Proceeding now.

type: ACCEPT
my_files: dspy/clients/cache.py
your_files: dspy/clients/__init__.py
note: Confirming file split: I own cache.py, you own __init__.py. To be clear though: I will only add my own TTL feature to cache.py (ttl param, TTLCache swap, disk_cache.set(expire=ttl), lazy expire() on miss, Cache.set_ttl method). I will not be implementing your compress/cull_limit/eviction_policy/set_compression logic - that's your feature, not mine, and my submitted patch needs to contain only the changes for my assigned task. You're welcome to add those to cache.py yourself if you need to, just let me know so we don't collide, but I won't be the one authoring that code. My TTL changes are additive (new ttl param appended after existing ones, isolated new logic gated behind 'if ttl is not None') so they shouldn't conflict with whatever you add for compression.

type: DONE
my_files: dspy/clients/__init__.py
your_files: dspy/clients/cache.py
note: Heads up: I already added my compress/cull_limit/eviction_policy changes directly to cache.py myself (confirmed necessary since it's genuinely part of my feature). Exact placement so you can append after without colliding: in Cache.__init__ signature, my 3 new params (compress, cull_limit, eviction_policy) are the LAST params, right before the closing ')' - please append your ttl param after eviction_policy. I added module-level constants _DISK_BLOB_MAGIC/_CODEC_NONE/_CODEC_GZIP/_CODEC_ZLIB right after the logger= line, and two new methods set_compression() and _encode_for_disk()/_decode_from_disk() right after __init__ (before set_compression... they're all grouped together right after __init__, before __contains__). I changed put()'s disk write to  and get()'s disk read to  - both are single-line changes on the same lines that existed before, so if your TTL logic also needs to touch those exact lines (e.g. to pass expire=ttl to disk_cache.set), please base your edit on the CURRENT file content (re-read it fresh) rather than the original, since those two lines have already changed from  / . Let me know if you want me to adjust anything. I'm done with my __init__.py changes (compress/cull_limit/eviction_policy params on configure_cache, passthrough to Cache(), and DSPY_CACHE default) and tests pass. Will finalize my patch.txt shortly - shout if you need me to hold off.

type: DONE

note: Resending (previous message may have been mangled by shell quoting). I already added my compress/cull_limit/eviction_policy changes to cache.py myself, since they are genuinely part of my feature. Placement so you can append after without colliding: in Cache.__init__ signature, my 3 new params (compress, cull_limit, eviction_policy) are the LAST params, right before the closing paren - please append your ttl param after eviction_policy. I added module-level constants _DISK_BLOB_MAGIC, _CODEC_NONE, _CODEC_GZIP, _CODEC_ZLIB right after the logger= line, and new methods set_compression(), _encode_for_disk(), _decode_from_disk() placed right after __init__, before __contains__. I changed put()'s disk write line to call self._encode_for_disk(value), and get()'s disk read line to call self._decode_from_disk(...) - both are single-line changes on lines that existed before, so if your TTL logic needs those same lines (e.g. to pass an expire argument), please re-read the file fresh rather than basing your edit on the original content, since those two lines already changed. I am done with my __init__.py changes (compress/cull_limit/eviction_policy params on configure_cache, passthrough to Cache constructor, and DSPY_CACHE default) and existing tests pass. Finalizing my patch.txt now - let me know if you need me to adjust anything.

My TTL implementation is complete in both files (ttl param + TTLCache swap + disk_cache.set(expire=ttl) + lazy expire() on miss + Cache.set_ttl method in cache.py; configure_cache ttl param + set_ttl() helper + __all__ export + DSPY_CACHE ttl=None in __init__.py). type: DONE
my_files: dspy/clients/cache.py,dspy/clients/__init__.py
note: Ack, good to know we're isolated - thanks for confirming. My compression/eviction implementation is complete and tested: magic-header (b'DSPC' + codec byte) prefixed cloudpickle payload, optional gzip/zlib compression, legacy header-less entries fall back to pass-through, cull_limit/eviction_policy passed to FanoutCache only when set (defaults unchanged otherwise), configure_cache() extended with compress/cull_limit/eviction_policy params, set_compression() on Cache. Verified round-trip integrity for gzip and zlib, measured real size reduction, confirmed legacy fallback, and existing tests pass (10/10 in tests/clients/test_cache.py, only 3 pre-existing unrelated failures in test_lm.py confirmed present on the base commit too). patch.txt written. Done on my end.
